title: Noun + はどうであれ
titleEn: This phrase means 'regardless of' or 'no matter how…'.
explanation: This phrase means 'regardless of' or 'no matter how…'. (Ngu?n: Hanabira; b?n d?ch Vi?t c?n bi?n t?p.)
explanationEn: It is used to convey indifference about the state or condition of a certain thing or matter. You can think of it as 'no matter what' or 'regardless of' in English. The phrase shows the speaker’s strong intention to do something or an assertion that doesn’t change regardless of the noun’s state or condition.

title: Noun まみれ
titleEn: Expresses a state in which something is entirely covered or filled with something else.
explanation: Expresses a state in which something is entirely covered or filled with something else. (Ngu?n: Hanabira; b?n d?ch Vi?t c?n bi?n t?p.)
explanationEn: The まみれ grammar point is used to express a state where someone or something is completely covered, smeared, or filled with another thing. The noun before まみれ indicates what the person or thing is covered in. This grammar point often has a negative connotation.

title: Noun もさることながら Noun も
titleEn: Means 'not only... but also'; the second noun is especially emphasized.
structure: Noun1 + もさることながら + Noun2 + も
structureEn: Noun1 + もさることながら + Noun2 + も
explanation: Means 'not only... but also'; the second noun is especially emphasized. (Ngu?n: Hanabira; b?n d?ch Vi?t c?n bi?n t?p.)
explanationEn: The grammar point 'Noun もさることながら Noun も' is used to express that both Nouns are true, important, or relevant. It can be translated as 'not only... but also' in English. However, among the two nouns, the one coming after もさることながら is more emphasized in comparison to the one before it.

title: Noun も兼ねて
titleEn: Used to express 'as well as', 'also', or 'at the same time'.
explanation: Used to express 'as well as', 'also', or 'at the same time'. (Ngu?n: Hanabira; b?n d?ch Vi?t c?n bi?n t?p.)
explanationEn: The も兼ねて grammar point indicates that two actions or purposes are being served simultaneously. The noun before も兼ねて tells what the additional action or purpose is.

title: Noun も相まって
titleEn: Used to express that combined with something else, it caused a certain outcome.
explanation: Used to express that combined with something else, it caused a certain outcome. (Ngu?n: Hanabira; b?n d?ch Vi?t c?n bi?n t?p.)
explanationEn: The grammar point Noun も相まって is used to express the meaning that, combined with something else, it caused a certain result. Literally, it translates to 'also contributes to'. It is generally used with nouns and points out that there are multiple contributing factors.

title: Noun を おいて他に Verb ない
titleEn: Means 'apart from ~ there's nothing else,' implying the Noun is the best or most suitable choice.
structure: Noun + をおいて他に + Verb negative form
structureEn: Noun + をおいて他に + Verb negative form
explanation: Means 'apart from ~ there's nothing else,' implying the Noun is the best or most suitable choice. (Ngu?n: Hanabira; b?n d?ch Vi?t c?n bi?n t?p.)
explanationEn: The structure Nounをおいて他に〜ない is used to express that there is no substitute for the Noun, no other choice, or that the Noun is the best or the only one to choose among various options. This form is often used in praising or making strong positive comments about someone or something.

title: Noun をもって
titleEn: Used to indicate means, methods, or time; 'with', 'by means of', 'at'.
explanation: Used to indicate means, methods, or time; 'with', 'by means of', 'at'. (Ngu?n: Hanabira; b?n d?ch Vi?t c?n bi?n t?p.)
explanationEn: The をもって grammar is used to express a means or method to achieve something, or to indicate a point in time. It can be translated as 'with' or 'by means of' when indicating means, and 'at' when indicating a point in time.

title: Noun をものともせずに
titleEn: Expresses the idea that 'despite something, action was carried out', translating to 'without being fazed by something'.
structure: Noun + をものともせずに
structureEn: Noun + をものともせずに
explanation: Expresses the idea that 'despite something, action was carried out', translating to 'without being fazed by something'. (Ngu?n: Hanabira; b?n d?ch Vi?t c?n bi?n t?p.)
explanationEn: The 'Nounをものともせずに' grammar point indicates that something which could potentially be a hindrance or challenge was not taken into account when carrying out an action. The literal idea is ‘not making something an obstacle’, meaning actions were performed without being fazed or distracted by it.

title: Noun をよそに
explanation: Despite; Notwithstanding; Regardless of (Ngu?n: Hanabira; b?n d?ch Vi?t c?n bi?n t?p.)
explanationEn: The Japanese phrase 'Nounをよそに' is used to express a situation where, despite some circumstance (represented by the noun), a certain action takes place. It can convey a feeling of disregard or nonchalance. In English, it can be translated as 'despite', 'notwithstanding', or 'regardless of'.

title: Noun を余儀なくされる
titleEn: Expresses the idea of being forced into a certain situation, 'to be compelled', 'to be obliged'.
structure: Noun + を + 余儀なくされる
structureEn: Noun + を + 余儀なくされる
explanation: Expresses the idea of being forced into a certain situation, 'to be compelled', 'to be obliged'. (Ngu?n: Hanabira; b?n d?ch Vi?t c?n bi?n t?p.)
explanationEn: The grammar pattern 'Noun を余儀なくされる' is used to describe circumstances where a person or party is compelled or obliged to do something due to necessity or unavoidable circumstances. It translates as 'to be forced to', 'to be compelled to', or 'to have no choice but to'. This form is often used in formal or written Japanese.
